PEHA.rule_description: The 5-digit product code is derived from the PN by taking the first 6 digits of the PN (left-padded with zeros if needed) and appending the check digit calculated from those 6 digits. One exception exists where PN 775511 maps to product code 79551 instead of the expected pattern.
PEHA.python_formula: lambda pn: (pn.zfill(8)[:6] + str((10 - sum((3 if i % 2 else 1) * int(d) for i, d in enumerate(pn.zfill(8)[:6])) % 10) % 10))

exceptions.reason: Product code is 79551 instead of expected 77551. Appears to use a different first digit (7→9) in the product code portion.

PEHA.applicable_to: Numeric PNs of 6-8 digits. Pattern takes first 6 digits of PN (left-padded) plus a check digit to form the 5-digit product code, with one known exception for PN 775511.

Honeywell.rule_description: No consistent rule found. Each PN appears to map to a different company prefix (first 7 digits of EAN vary: 4025515, 4046356, 7312550, 4017918, 5017490, 4015120, 2050000). The three PNs EP10425882, EP10427213, and EP10427404 all map to the same EAN (4015120423093), suggesting these are aliases or variants. No mathematical relationship found between PN and the 5-digit product code portion.
Honeywell.python_formula: No formula - appears to be lookup table mapping

exceptions.reason: Company prefix 4025515 - unique to this PN
Company prefix 4046356 - unique to this PN
Company prefix 7312550 - shared with LCD only
Company prefix 4017918 - unique to this PN
Company prefix 5017490 - shared with IP only
Company prefix 4015120 - shared with EP10427213 and EP10427404 (same EAN)
Duplicate EAN - same as EP10425882 and EP10427404
Duplicate EAN - same as EP10425882 and EP10427213
Company prefix 5017490 - shared with EASY only
Company prefix 7312550 - shared with COLOR only
Company prefix 2050000 - unique to this PN

Honeywell.applicable_to: None - no algorithmic pattern exists. This appears to be a lookup table where different Honeywell products are sourced from different manufacturers/distributors, each with their own GS1 company prefix.

ABB.rule_description: The product code appears to be derived from a hash or internal mapping system. No direct pattern found from PN characters to the 5-digit product code. The PNs follow different formats (2CDG110146R0011, 2CDG110177R0011 are long alphanumeric codes, CR-MH is short) but all share the same company prefix 4016779.
ABB.python_formula: PN_TO_PRODUCT_CODE: lambda pn: LOOKUP_TABLE.get(pn, '00000') # Requires mapping table

exceptions.reason: No mathematical or positional relationship found between PN and product code 86669
No mathematical or positional relationship found between PN and product code 90651
Different company prefix (4013614 vs 4016779), suggests different product line or exception case

ABB.applicable_to: Unknown - insufficient data to determine algorithmic pattern. CR-MH uses different prefix entirely (4013614 vs 4016779). Requires lookup table or more samples to identify pattern.

Howard Leight.rule_description: The 5-digit product code is derived by taking the last 5 digits of the PN. The company prefix varies: 7312550 is used when the 2nd digit of PN is 0, and 7312553 is used when the 2nd digit of PN is 0 (both examples have 0 as 2nd digit, but different prefixes). Actually, the prefix appears to encode information from the PN: 7312553 for PN starting with 1000xxx, 7312550 for PN starting with 1006xxx.
